session.user: Run pytest on tests/commands/one_shot/test_one_shot.py -v and report results. Just run the command and report what passes/fails.
session.assistant: I'll run pytest on the specified test file and report the results.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Run pytest on one_shot tests with verbose output

session.tool_result:
============================= test session starts ============================== platform linux -- Python 3.13.12, pytest-9.0.2, pluggy-1.6.0 rootdir: /home/runner/work/erk/erk configfile: pyproject.toml plugins: anyio-4.12.1, cov-7.0.0, xdist-3.8.0, asyncio-1.3.0 asyncio: mode=Mode.AUTO, debug=False, asyncio_default_fixture_loop_scope=function, asyncio_default_test_loop_scope=function collected 12 items
tests/commands/one_shot/test_one_shot.py ............ [100%]
============================== 12 passed in 0.77s ==============================
